definitions.title: Associative algebra
definitions.notes: Mathlib's `Algebra R A` typeclass models an associative R-algebra. Over a field k, `Algebra k A` with `Ring A` gives exactly an associative k-algebra.

definitions.title: Unit in an associative algebra
definitions.notes: Mathlib algebras are unital by default (`Ring A` includes `One A`). The unit is built into the `Ring` structure.

definitions.title: Commutative algebra
definitions.notes: `Algebra k A` with `CommRing A` gives a commutative k-algebra.

definitions.title: Homomorphism of algebras
definitions.notes: `AlgHom R A B` (notation `A →ₐ[R] B`) is exactly a homomorphism of R-algebras. `AlgEquiv` for isomorphisms.

definitions.title: Representation of an algebra (left A-module)
definitions.notes: A left A-module is `Module A V`. For representation-theoretic context, `Representation k G V` exists for group representations. For algebra representations, `Module A V` is the standard approach.

definitions.title: Subrepresentation
definitions.notes: `Submodule A V` is the type of A-submodules of V, i.e., subrepresentations.

definitions.title: Irreducible (simple) representation
definitions.notes: `IsSimpleModule R M` asserts that the module M has exactly two submodules (⊥ and ⊤), i.e., is irreducible/simple.

definitions.title: Homomorphism (intertwining operator) of representations
definitions.notes: `LinearMap` (notation `M →ₗ[R] N`) is an R-module homomorphism. For group representations, `Representation.linHom` and related constructions exist.

definitions.title: Direct sum of representations
definitions.notes: `DirectSum ι M` gives the direct sum of a family of modules. Binary direct sum via `M × N` with product module structure.

definitions.title: Indecomposable representation
definitions.notes: Mathlib has `Indecomposable` for order theory. For modules, indecomposability (not expressible as direct sum of proper submodules) may need to be defined using `IsNontrivial` and non-existence of complementary submodule pairs. Partial coverage.

definitions.title: Faithful representation
definitions.notes: `FaithfulSMul A V` asserts that the action is faithful, i.e., the representation map A → End(V) is injective.

definitions.title: Quiver
definitions.notes: `Quiver V` is a typeclass providing `Hom : V → V → Sort*`. Mathlib's quiver is exactly a directed graph (possibly with self-loops and multiple edges).

definitions.title: Representation of a quiver
definitions.notes: Mathlib has `Representation k G V` for group representations. Quiver representations can be modeled as functors from the free category on the quiver to `ModuleCat k`. Specific quiver representation API exists but naming may vary.

definitions.title: Path algebra of a quiver
definitions.notes: Mathlib has `Quiver.Path` (composable sequences of arrows) and path categories. The full path algebra as a k-algebra may need construction from `FreeAlgebra` or `MonoidAlgebra` over the path monoid. Partial coverage.

definitions.title: Subrepresentation of a quiver representation
definitions.notes: Subrepresentations of quiver representations would be subfunctors. Mathlib has general subfunctor infrastructure but specific quiver subrepresentation support is limited.

definitions.title: Direct sum of quiver representations
definitions.notes: Direct sums of functors exist in general category theory. Specific quiver representation direct sums may need construction.

definitions.title: Homomorphism of quiver representations
definitions.notes: Morphisms of quiver representations are natural transformations. Mathlib has `NatTrans` but specific quiver representation morphism API may be limited.

definitions.title: Lie algebra
definitions.notes: `LieAlgebra R L` with `LieRing L` gives a Lie algebra over R. The Jacobi identity is built into `LieRing`.

definitions.title: Homomorphism of Lie algebras
definitions.notes: `LieHom R L₁ L₂` (notation `L₁ →ₗ⁅R⁆ L₂`) is a Lie algebra homomorphism.

definitions.title: Representation of a Lie algebra
definitions.notes: `LieModule R L M` gives a Lie algebra representation (Lie module structure on M).

definitions.title: Universal enveloping algebra
definitions.notes: `UniversalEnvelopingAlgebra R L` is the universal enveloping algebra of a Lie algebra L over R.

definitions.title: Tensor product of vector spaces
definitions.notes: `TensorProduct R M N` (notation `M ⊗[R] N`) is the tensor product of R-modules M and N.

definitions.title: Symmetric algebra, exterior algebra, and universal enveloping algebra (coordinate-free)
definitions.notes: All three are in Mathlib: `SymmetricAlgebra R M`, `ExteriorAlgebra R M`, and `UniversalEnvelopingAlgebra R L`.

definitions.title: Tensor product of Lie algebra representations
definitions.notes: Mathlib has both `TensorProduct` and `LieModule`. The Lie module structure on tensor products (via the Leibniz rule) may exist under `Mathlib.Algebra.Lie.TensorProduct` or similar. Exact instance name needs verification.

definitions.title: Dual representation of a Lie algebra
definitions.notes: The dual of a Lie module uses `Module.Dual R M` with the contragredient Lie action. Mathlib has `LieModule.dualLieModule` or equivalent constructions.

definitions.title: Semisimple (completely reducible) representation
definitions.notes: `IsSemisimpleModule R M` asserts that every submodule has a complement, i.e., M is completely reducible.

definitions.title: Dual representation
definitions.notes: `Module.Dual R M` is the dual vector space. For group representations, `Representation.dual` gives the contragredient.

definitions.title: Filtration of a representation
definitions.notes: Mathlib has `CompositionSeries` for Jordan-Hölder filtrations. General filtrations (ascending chain of submodules) can be modeled as `ℕ →o Submodule R M` but there's no dedicated `Filtration` type for modules.

definitions.title: Radical of a finite dimensional algebra
definitions.notes: The Jacobson radical is `Ideal.jacobson ⊥` (intersection of all maximal left ideals). For finite dimensional algebras this equals the radical.

definitions.title: Semisimple algebra
definitions.notes: `IsSemisimpleRing A` asserts that A is a semisimple ring (every module is semisimple).

definitions.title: Unitary representation
definitions.notes: Mathlib has `UnitaryGroup` and inner product spaces, but a dedicated `UnitaryRepresentation` (representation preserving an inner product) type is not available. Can be modeled using `Representation` plus a compatibility condition with `InnerProductSpace`.

definitions.title: Frobenius determinant (group determinant)
definitions.notes: The group determinant (determinant of the matrix X_G with entries x_{g_i g_j}) is not in Mathlib. Needs to be defined from scratch using `Matrix.det` and group algebra structure.

definitions.title: Complex, real, and quaternionic type of irreducible representations
definitions.notes: The classification of irreducible representations into complex/real/quaternionic types (based on V ≅ V* and existence of invariant bilinear forms) is not in Mathlib. Needs to be defined from scratch.

definitions.title: Frobenius-Schur indicator
definitions.notes: The Frobenius-Schur indicator (0 for complex type, 1 for real, -1 for quaternionic) is not in Mathlib. Needs to be defined from scratch.

definitions.title: Algebraic number and algebraic integer via monic polynomials
definitions.notes: `IsAlgebraic R x` (x is a root of a nonzero polynomial over R) and `IsIntegral R x` (x is a root of a monic polynomial over R) exactly match these definitions.

definitions.title: Algebraic number and algebraic integer via eigenvalues of matrices
definitions.notes: The characterization via eigenvalues of integer matrices is a theorem (equivalence), not a separate definition in Mathlib. The definitions exist but the eigenvalue characterization may need proof.

definitions.title: Solvable group
definitions.notes: `IsSolvable G` asserts that G is solvable (derived series terminates at 1).

definitions.title: Virtual representation and its character
definitions.notes: Virtual representations (formal differences of representations, elements of the representation ring/Grothendieck group) are not in Mathlib. The representation ring `RepresentationRing` would need to be constructed as a Grothendieck group.

definitions.title: Induced representation Ind_H^G V
definitions.notes: Mathlib has `Representation k G V` for group representations and `MonoidAlgebra k G` for group algebras. The induced representation `Ind_H^G V = k[G] ⊗[k[H]] V` can be constructed from these components. A dedicated `Representation.induced` may not exist but the building blocks are available.

definitions.title: Partition, Young diagram, Young tableau, row and column subgroups
definitions.notes: `Nat.Partition` for partitions, `YoungDiagram` for Young diagrams, `SemistandardYoungTableau` and `StandardYoungTableau` for tableaux. Row and column subgroups may need custom definition.

definitions.title: Kostka numbers K_{mu,lambda}
definitions.notes: Kostka numbers (number of semistandard Young tableaux of shape λ and weight μ) are not in Mathlib. Needs definition using `SemistandardYoungTableau`.

definitions.title: Algebraic (rational, polynomial) representation of GL(V)
definitions.notes: Polynomial/algebraic representations of GL(V) (where matrix coefficients are polynomial functions) are not in Mathlib. Needs to be defined from scratch.

definitions.title: Dynkin diagram
definitions.notes: Mathlib has `CoxeterMatrix` and `CoxeterSystem` for Coxeter-Dynkin data. The specific `DynkinDiagram` inductive type may not exist; instead Dynkin types are encoded via their Cartan/Coxeter matrices. Partial coverage — the classification of positive-definite graphs as Dynkin diagrams would need custom work.

definitions.title: Cartan matrix
definitions.notes: Cartan matrices can be derived from Coxeter matrices via `CoxeterMatrix`. The specific `cartanMatrix` function for Dynkin diagrams may need custom construction from the Coxeter data.

definitions.title: Root
definitions.notes: Mathlib has `RootPairing` which formalizes root systems abstractly. Individual roots are elements of the root pairing. The book's definition of roots of a quiver's quadratic form is more elementary and specific.

definitions.title: Simple roots
definitions.notes: Mathlib's `RootPairing` has simple roots via the indexing. The book's context (simple roots of quiver root systems) is more specialized.

definitions.title: Positive and negative roots
definitions.notes: Mathlib has positive root concepts via `RootPairing`. The book's definition (in terms of quiver dimension vectors) is more elementary and may need adaptation.

definitions.title: Reflection and simple reflections
definitions.notes: Mathlib has reflections in the context of root pairings via `RootPairing.reflection` and Weyl group elements.

definitions.title: Dimension vector
definitions.notes: The dimension vector of a quiver representation (assigning dim(V_i) to each vertex i) is not in Mathlib. Needs to be defined as a function from vertices to ℕ.

definitions.title: Sink and source
definitions.notes: Sink (vertex with all edges incoming) and source (all edges outgoing) for quivers are not defined in Mathlib. Need custom definitions.

definitions.title: Reversed quiver at a vertex
definitions.notes: The operation of reversing all edges at a single vertex of a quiver is not in Mathlib. Mathlib has `Quiver.reverse` for reversing all edges but not vertex-local reversal.

definitions.title: Reflection functor F_i^+
definitions.notes: Bernstein-Gelfand-Ponomarev reflection functors for quiver representations are not in Mathlib. Need to be defined from scratch.

definitions.title: Reflection functor F_i^-
definitions.notes: The dual reflection functor F_i^- (for sources) is not in Mathlib. Needs to be defined from scratch alongside F_i^+.

definitions.title: Coxeter element
definitions.notes: Mathlib has `CoxeterSystem` and `CoxeterGroup`. The Coxeter element (product of all simple reflections) as a specific element may need explicit construction.

definitions.title: Category
definitions.notes: `CategoryTheory.Category C` is exactly the standard definition of a category.

definitions.title: Full subcategory
definitions.notes: `InducedCategory D F` gives a category on a type C induced by a function F : C → D. A full subcategory is modeled by combining `InducedCategory` with `Functor.Full` (the property that the functor on Hom sets is surjective).

definitions.title: Functor
definitions.notes: `CategoryTheory.Functor C D` is a functor from C to D.

definitions.title: Natural transformation
definitions.notes: `CategoryTheory.NatTrans F G` is a natural transformation from functor F to functor G.

definitions.title: Equivalence of categories
definitions.notes: `CategoryTheory.Equivalence C D` is an equivalence of categories.

definitions.title: Adjoint functors
definitions.notes: `CategoryTheory.Adjunction F G` is an adjunction between functors F and G.

definitions.title: Abelian category
definitions.notes: `CategoryTheory.Abelian C` asserts that C is an abelian category.

definitions.title: Complex, differentials, cohomology, exact sequence
definitions.notes: `HomologicalComplex C c` for chain/cochain complexes, with `homology` functor. Exact sequences via `CategoryTheory.Exact`.

definitions.title: Short exact sequence
definitions.notes: `ShortComplex C` and `ShortExact` model short exact sequences in abelian categories.

definitions.title: Connecting homomorphism and long exact sequence
definitions.notes: Mathlib has the snake lemma and connecting homomorphisms for deriving long exact sequences from short exact sequences of complexes.

definitions.title: Additive and k-linear functor
definitions.notes: `Functor.Additive F` for additive functors, `Functor.Linear R F` for R-linear functors.

definitions.title: Left exact, right exact, and exact functor
definitions.notes: Left exact = preserves finite limits (`PreservesFiniteLimits`), right exact = preserves finite colimits (`PreservesFiniteColimits`). Exact = both.

definitions.title: Semisimple abelian category
definitions.notes: Mathlib has abelian categories but does not have a dedicated `IsSemisimple` predicate for categories (every object is semisimple). For module categories, `IsSemisimpleRing` implies the category is semisimple.

definitions.title: Projective module
definitions.notes: `Module.Projective R M` asserts that M is a projective R-module.

definitions.title: Injective module
definitions.notes: `Module.Injective R M` asserts that M is an injective R-module.

definitions.title: Projective and injective objects in abelian categories
definitions.notes: `CategoryTheory.Projective X` and `CategoryTheory.Injective X` for objects in categories with enough projectives/injectives.

definitions.title: Projective resolution
definitions.notes: `CategoryTheory.ProjectiveResolution X` is a projective resolution of X in an abelian category.

definitions.title: Tor functors
definitions.notes: Mathlib has `Tor` in `Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.DerivedCategory.Basic` but the API may be limited. The derived functor construction exists but concrete Tor computations may need work.

definitions.title: Ext functors
definitions.notes: Mathlib has `Ext R M N` for Ext groups, defined via derived functors of Hom.

definitions.title: Complete system of orthogonal idempotents
definitions.notes: Mathlib has `OrthogonalIdempotents` and `CompleteOrthogonalIdempotents` for systems of orthogonal idempotents that sum to 1.

definitions.title: Projective cover
definitions.notes: Projective covers (dual of injective envelopes) are not explicitly in Mathlib as a dedicated type, though `CategoryTheory.InjectiveResolution` exists. May need custom definition as an essential epimorphism from a projective.

definitions.title: Cartan matrix (of a finite dimensional algebra)
definitions.notes: The Cartan matrix of a finite dimensional algebra (matrix of multiplicities of simple modules in projective indecomposable modules) is not in Mathlib. Different from `DynkinDiagram.cartanMatrix`. Needs to be defined from scratch.

definitions.title: Projective dimension
definitions.notes: Projective dimension (shortest projective resolution length) is not in Mathlib. Needs to be defined from scratch.

definitions.title: Homological dimension of a ring
definitions.notes: Global homological dimension (supremum of projective dimensions of all modules) is not in Mathlib. Needs to be defined from scratch.

definitions.title: Linked simple modules and blocks
definitions.notes: The linkage relation on simple modules (via nonvanishing Ext^1) and the resulting block decomposition of module categories are not in Mathlib. Need to be defined from scratch.

definitions.title: Finite abelian category
definitions.notes: Mathlib has abelian categories but not the specific finiteness conditions (finite-dimensional Hom spaces, finite length objects, finitely many simples, enough projectives). Would need a custom class combining these conditions.

definitions.title: Projective generator (progenerator)
definitions.notes: Mathlib has `IsGenerator` for objects in categories. A progenerator (projective generator) would combine `Projective` and `IsGenerator`. Not available as a single concept.

definitions.title: Morita equivalence
definitions.notes: Morita equivalence (equivalence of module categories) can be expressed as `CategoryTheory.Equivalence (ModuleCat R) (ModuleCat S)`. No dedicated `MoritaEquivalent` predicate exists.

definitions.title: Basic algebra
definitions.notes: A basic algebra (one where all simple modules are 1-dimensional, or equivalently A/rad(A) is a product of copies of k) is not in Mathlib. Needs to be defined from scratch.
